item.title: Chipmakers in Malaysia monitoring risks from helium supply disruptions, association says
item.description: Semiconductor firms in Malaysia are monitoring risks from disruptions to helium supplies due to the conflict in the Middle ​East, though the situation has not caused any operational interruptions so ‌far, an industry executive told Reuters.

item.title: A mystery AI model has developers buzzing: Is this DeepSeek's latest blockbuster?
item.description: A powerful artificial intelligence model that appeared anonymously on a developer platform last week has sparked speculation that Chinese startup DeepSeek may be quietly testing its next-generation system ahead of an official launch.

item.title: Samsung Elec plans to produce Tesla chips starting late 2027
item.description: Samsung Electronics ​said on Wednesday ‌that it expects to ​start ​volume production of Tesla's ⁠chips ​at Samsung's factory ​in Texas in the second ​half of ​next year.

item.title: Asian stocks rally as oil retreats, Fed in spotlight
item.description: Asian shares rallied on Wednesday as oil prices paused their gains, with markets turning to the U.S. Federal Reserve meeting to see ​how policymakers will balance growth and inflation risks amid ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

item.title: Elliott Management confirms stake in Japan shipper Mitsui OSK
item.description: Elliott ​Investment Management ‌said on ​Wednesday ​that it ⁠has ​a built ​a "significant" investment in ​Japanese ​shipping company ‌Mitsui ⁠OSK Lines , confirming ​an ​earlier Reuters ⁠report.

item.title: Chinese authorities approve Nvidia's H200 AI chip sales, source says
item.description: Chinese authorities have granted approval for multiple Chinese companies ​to purchase H200 AI chips from Nvidia, ‌according to a person familiar with the situation.

item.title: AMD CEO Lisa Su to visit Samsung chip plant in South Korea, discuss expanding ties, source says
item.description: Advanced Micro Devices CEO Lisa Su is set to ​visit Samsung Electronics' chip production site ‌in Pyeongtaek, South Korea on Wednesday, where she will tour production lines and discuss ​expanding cooperation beyond memory into ​foundry manufacturing, a source with knowledge ⁠of the trip said.

item.title: Oil prices drop as U.S. crude inventories show an increase
item.description: Oil prices slightly fell on Wednesday morning after sources citing American Petroleum Institute figures showed an ​increase in U.S. crude inventories.

item.title: Samsung sees AI driving strong chip demand in 2026, executive says
item.description: Samsung Electronics ​sees strong demand ‌for chips continuing this year, driven ​by the ​global artificial intelligence wave, ⁠but rising ​memory chip prices could ​hit computer and mobile shipments, its top ​executive said ​on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump administration defends Anthropic blacklisting in US court
item.description: The Trump administration said in a Tuesday court filing that the Pentagon’s blacklisting of Anthropic was justified and lawful, opposing ​the artificial intelligence lab’s high-stakes lawsuit challenging the decision.

item.title: Dollar holds losses as risk appetite flickers ahead of central bank meetings
item.description: The dollar held losses on Wednesday as a glimmer of risk appetite came back to markets ahead of a slate of key central bank decisions.

item.title: With watchful eye on Iran war, crypto dodges UAE disruption
item.description: Laia Fernández has been working as normal as a crypto marketing executive from her high-rise apartment in Dubai's gleaming downtown business district, even as occasional sounds ​of projectile interceptions act as reminders of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

item.title: Musk's motives are debated as Twitter shareholder trial nears end
item.description: A lawyer for former Twitter shareholders suing Elon Musk urged a federal jury on Tuesday to ​hold the world's richest person liable for driving down Twitter's stock price in 2022 by attempting to back out of or ‌renegotiate his $44 billion purchase of the social media platform.

item.title: Brazilian truckers weigh strike as diesel prices jump amid Middle East conflict
item.description: Truck drivers' unions in Brazil are advocating for a strike as early as this week after the recent jump in ​diesel prices due to the conflict in the Middle East, ‌a union head said on Tuesday.

item.title: Fintech lender SoFi disputes short seller Muddy Waters' report, considers legal action
item.description: California-based ​fintech lender SoFi Technologies said on ‌Tuesday that it may explore legal action against short seller Muddy Waters Research, calling ​its report "factually inaccurate and misleading."

item.title: Top Japan firms set to offer big pay hikes, focus on Iran conflict ahead
item.description: Many major companies are set to offer big pay hikes as they wrap up their annual wage ​talks with unions on Wednesday, reflecting strong pay momentum ‌for a fourth consecutive year, although uncertainty from the Middle East conflict clouds the outlook.

item.title: BP locks out union workers at its Midwest refinery
item.description: BP on Tuesday barred about 800 ​United Steelworkers members ‌from its 440,000 barrel-per-day refinery in Whiting, ​Indiana starting at ​12:00 a.m. on March ⁠19 after negotiations ​with the union broke ​down.

item.title: Exclusive: Nvidia preparing Groq chips that can be sold in Chinese market, sources say
item.description: Nvidia is preparing a ​version of its Groq artificial-intelligence chips that can be sold to ‌the Chinese market, two sources familiar with the matter told Reuters on Tuesday.

item.title: US Postal Service could run out of money as soon as October
item.description: The head of the U.S. Postal Service told Congress on Tuesday it could run out of ​money in October or November if the agency continues to make required ‌retirement and other payments to the government.

item.title: Amazon plans drastic cut in packages it sends through post office, WSJ reports
item.description: Amazon.com is looking to sharply cut the ​number of packages it ‌sends through the U.S. Postal Service, a move that ​could cost the agency ​millions of dollars in revenue, ⁠the Wall Street ​Journal reported on Tuesday.

item.title: Australia's Woodside names Liz Westcott as new CEO
item.description: Australia's ​Woodside ‌Energy ​named ​Liz ⁠Westcott ​as ​its new ​chief ​executive officer ‌and ⁠managing ​director ​on ⁠Wednesday.

item.title: FTC monitoring how drug companies react to patent cliff, official says
item.description: The ​U.S. Federal Trade Commission is on the lookout for anticompetitive practices in the pharmaceutical ‌industry with a number of blockbuster drugs near the end of their patents, the FTC's head of antitrust enforcement said on Tuesday, as the agency looks to protect consumers from unfair prices.

item.title: US gasoline prices soar past $3.75 a gallon as Middle East war rages on
item.description: The U.S. national average price for gasoline surpassed $3.75 a gallon for the first time since October ​2023 on Tuesday, GasBuddy data showed, as global fuel ‌markets continue to reel under supply disruptions caused by the Middle East war.

item.title: BHP names Brandon Craig as CEO, succeeding Mike Henry
item.description: Mining giant BHP ​Group on Wednesday named Brandon Craig as ‌CEO to succeed Mike Henry, who will step down after more than six years at ​the helm.

item.title: Trading Day: Oil back above $100... and so?
item.description: Stocks rose, and bond yields and the dollar slipped on Tuesday, as investors shrugged off a rebound in oil prices that lifted Brent crude back above $100 a barrel, and turned their attention to the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision on Wednesday.

item.title: US securities regulator issues long-awaited crypto guidance
item.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission on Tuesday issued an interpretation clarifying which types of cryptocurrencies are considered securities and how a "non-security" ​digital asset could meet certain conditions to become an investment contract.

item.title: Lululemon sees annual revenue, profit below estimates amid board, demand challenges
item.description: Athletic apparel ​maker Lululemon forecast 2026 revenue ‌and profit below Wall Street expectations on Tuesday, underscoring its challenges as it faces ​rising competition and an attempt ​by founder Chip Wilson to overhaul ⁠the board.

item.title: Nvidia sales opportunity for Blackwell, Rubin chips more than $1 trillion by 2027
item.description: Nvidia CEO ​Jensen Huang said ‌on Tuesday at a press conference ​that the revenue ​opportunity for the company's ⁠Blackwell and ​Rubin artificial intelligence ​chips is "likely to be larger than $1 trillion" by ​the end ​of 2027. The estimate does ‌not ⁠include the company's networking chips and the new ​processors made ​with ⁠technology from the Groq ​licensing deal it ​signed ⁠in December.

item.title: Exclusive: Amazon CEO sees AI doubling prior AWS sales projections to $600 billion by 2036
item.description: Amazon O CEO Andy Jassy ​said during an internal all-hands meeting ‌he expects artificial intelligence could help cloud computing unit Amazon Web Services achieve $600 billion in annual sales, ​double his own prior estimate.

item.title: Elon Musk, US SEC in talks to settle lawsuit over Twitter disclosures
item.description: Elon Musk and the ​U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission are in ‌talks to settle the regulator's lawsuit accusing the world's richest person of waiting too long to ​disclose his purchases of Twitter shares ​in 2022.
